timing_rules.advice: Take on an empty stomach, 30-60 min before breakfast.
timing_rules.reason: Food reduces absorption by up to 80%.

timing_rules.advice: Take simvastatin/lovastatin in the evening. Atorvastatin/rosuvastatin can be taken any time.
timing_rules.reason: Short-acting statins are more effective when taken at night (cholesterol synthesis peaks overnight).

timing_rules.advice: Take on an empty stomach with vitamin C for best absorption. If GI upset, take with a small amount of food.
timing_rules.reason: Vitamin C converts ferric to ferrous iron, increasing absorption.

timing_rules.advice: Take with meals. Split into 500mg doses (max absorption per dose).
timing_rules.reason: Stomach acid from meals aids calcium carbonate absorption.

timing_rules.advice: Take in the evening. Magnesium glycinate preferred for sleep.
timing_rules.reason: May promote relaxation and sleep. Oxide form can cause GI issues.

timing_rules.advice: Take with a fat-containing meal for better absorption.
timing_rules.reason: Vitamin D is fat-soluble — absorption improves 50% with dietary fat.

timing_rules.advice: Take with meals to reduce GI side effects.
timing_rules.reason: Taking with food improves absorption and reduces fishy burps.

timing_rules.advice: Take with a fat-containing meal. Ubiquinol form is better absorbed.
timing_rules.reason: CoQ10 is fat-soluble. Ubiquinol is the reduced (active) form.

timing_rules.advice: Take on an empty stomach for best absorption. If nausea, take with a small meal (not dairy).
timing_rules.reason: Food (especially phytates and dairy) reduces zinc absorption.

timing_rules.advice: Take 30-60 minutes before the first meal of the day.
timing_rules.reason: PPIs block acid pumps activated by eating. Must be taken before food.

timing_rules.advice: Take with meals to reduce GI side effects.
timing_rules.reason: Food slows absorption and reduces nausea/diarrhea.

timing_rules.advice: Take at the same time daily. Can be morning or evening.
timing_rules.reason: Consistency matters more than specific time of day.

timing_rules.advice: Take at the same time daily. Some evidence for evening dosing.
timing_rules.reason: Evening dosing may better control overnight/morning BP surge.

timing_rules.advice: Rivaroxaban: take with food (increases absorption 40%). Apixaban/dabigatran: with or without food.
timing_rules.reason: Rivaroxaban bioavailability drops significantly without food.

timing_rules.advice: Take 2 hours before or 6 hours after antacids, iron, calcium, magnesium, or zinc. Avoid dairy within 2 hours.
timing_rules.reason: Divalent/trivalent cations form insoluble chelates reducing absorption by 90%.

timing_rules.advice: Take on empty stomach, 1 hour before or 2 hours after meals. Avoid dairy, antacids, iron for 2-3 hours.
timing_rules.reason: Chelation with Ca²⁺, Mg²⁺, Fe²⁺, Al³⁺ dramatically reduces absorption.

timing_rules.advice: Take 30-60 minutes before bedtime. Start with 0.5-1mg.
timing_rules.reason: Mimics endogenous melatonin timing. Higher doses are not more effective.

timing_rules.advice: Take with meals, 500mg 2-3x/day. Space doses throughout the day.
timing_rules.reason: Short half-life (~5 hours). Taking with food reduces GI side effects.

timing_rules.advice: Take with meals or 30 minutes before eating.
timing_rules.reason: Meal-buffered stomach acid improves probiotic survival to intestines.

timing_rules.advice: Take on an empty stomach for best absorption, or with meals if GI upset.
timing_rules.reason: N-acetylcysteine absorption is reduced by food.

timing_rules.advice: Weekly injectables (semaglutide, dulaglutide): same day each week. Eat slowly and stop when full — delayed gastric emptying.
timing_rules.reason: GLP-1 agonists slow gastric emptying. Eating too fast increases nausea.

timing_rules.advice: Take in the morning. Stay hydrated throughout the day.
timing_rules.reason: SGLT2 inhibitors increase urinary glucose and fluid loss. Morning dosing avoids nighttime urination.

interactions.mechanism: Grapefruit inhibits CYP3A4, dramatically increasing statin blood levels and risk of rhabdomyolysis.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid grapefruit/grapefruit juice with CYP3A4-metabolized statins (simvastatin, lovastatin, atorvastatin).
citations: Lilja JJ et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 1998;64(5):477-483.

interactions.mechanism: Statins may reduce endogenous CoQ10 synthesis via HMG-CoA reductase inhibition.
interactions.recommendation: CoQ10 supplementation is generally safe. Consider if experiencing muscle symptoms.
citations: Caso G et al. Am J Cardiol. 2007;99(10):1409-1412.

interactions.mechanism: St. John's Wort induces CYP3A4, potentially reducing statin efficacy.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid combining. Monitor lipid levels if already taking both.
citations: Sugimoto K et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2001;70(6):518-524.

interactions.mechanism: Vitamin K directly antagonizes warfarin by providing substrate for clotting factor synthesis.
interactions.recommendation: Maintain consistent vitamin K intake. Do not start/stop supplements without INR monitoring.
citations: Booth SL et al. J Thromb Haemost. 2004;2(10):1808-1813.
interactions.timing_advice: Keep daily vitamin K intake consistent (same foods, same amounts). Don't suddenly eat large amounts of leafy greens or stop eating them.

interactions.mechanism: Additive anticoagulant and antiplatelet effects significantly increase bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid unless specifically prescribed together. Monitor for signs of bleeding.
citations: Lopes RD et al. JAMA. 2018;319(7):686-692.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs inhibit platelet function and cause GI mucosal damage, greatly increasing bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs with warfarin. Use acetaminophen instead. If unavoidable, lowest dose + INR monitoring.
citations: Knijff-Dutmer EA et al. Ann Pharmacother. 2003;37(1):12-16.

interactions.mechanism: Omega-3 fatty acids may have mild antiplatelet/anticoagulant effects enhancing warfarin.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor INR more frequently when starting/changing fish oil dose. >3g/day increases risk.
citations: Buckley MS et al. Ann Pharmacother. 2004;38(1):50-52.

interactions.mechanism: Ginkgo biloba inhibits PAF, enhancing warfarin-related bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid ginkgo while on warfarin. Discontinue 2+ weeks before surgery.
citations: Engelsen J et al. Ugeskr Laeger. 2003;165(18):1868-1871.

interactions.mechanism: Curcumin may inhibit platelet aggregation and potentiate warfarin anticoagulation.
interactions.recommendation: Use caution. Monitor INR when adding or removing turmeric/curcumin supplements.
citations: Shah BH et al. Biochem Pharmacol. 1999;58(7):1167-1172.

interactions.mechanism: St. John's Wort strongly induces CYP2C9/CYP1A2, accelerating warfarin metabolism.
interactions.recommendation: Contraindicated. Can cause dangerous loss of anticoagulation. Do not combine.
citations: Jiang X et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2004;76(6):657-667.

interactions.mechanism: Grapefruit may mildly inhibit CYP metabolism of warfarin R-enantiomer.
interactions.recommendation: Moderate intake unlikely to cause issues. Monitor INR if consumption changes significantly.
citations: Taylor JR, Wilt VM. Pharmacotherapy. 1999;19(12):1439-1448.

interactions.mechanism: Calcium binds levothyroxine in the gut, forming insoluble complex reducing absorption.
interactions.recommendation: Separate doses by 4+ hours. Take levothyroxine on empty stomach, calcium with meals.
interactions.timing_advice: Take levothyroxine first thing in morning on empty stomach. Wait at least 4 hours before taking calcium.

interactions.mechanism: Iron salts form insoluble complexes with levothyroxine, reducing absorption by up to 65%.
interactions.recommendation: Separate doses by 4+ hours. Take levothyroxine in morning, iron later.
citations: Campbell NR et al. Ann Intern Med. 1992;117(12):1010-1013.
interactions.timing_advice: Take levothyroxine first thing in morning. Take iron at least 4 hours later (with lunch or dinner).

interactions.mechanism: Magnesium may chelate levothyroxine in the GI tract, reducing absorption.
interactions.recommendation: Separate doses by 4+ hours.
citations: Csako G et al. Thyroid. 2001;11(8):789-791.
interactions.timing_advice: Take levothyroxine in the morning. Take magnesium in the evening (at least 4 hours later).

interactions.mechanism: PPIs reduce gastric acid needed for levothyroxine dissolution and absorption.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor TSH when starting/stopping PPI. Dose may need to increase 20-40%.
citations: Irving SA et al. Pharmacotherapy. 2015;35(11):e210-e215.

interactions.mechanism: Zinc may mildly reduce levothyroxine absorption through chelation.
interactions.recommendation: Separate doses by 2-4 hours as a precaution.
citations: Civitelli R et al. Am J Clin Nutr. 2004;80(3):699-703.

interactions.mechanism: Alcohol potentiates metformin's effect on lactate metabolism, increasing lactic acidosis risk.
interactions.recommendation: Limit alcohol. Avoid binge drinking. Seek help for lactic acidosis symptoms.
citations: DeFronzo RA et al. N Engl J Med. 1995;333(9):574-579.

interactions.mechanism: ACE inhibitors reduce aldosterone, decreasing potassium excretion. Supplements cause hyperkalemia.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid potassium supplements unless prescribed and monitored. Check serum K+ regularly.
citations: Palmer BF. N Engl J Med. 2004;351(6):585-592.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal prostaglandins, blunting antihypertensive effect and increasing nephrotoxicity.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor BP and renal function. Use lowest effective NSAID dose for shortest duration.
citations: Catella-Lawson F et al. N Engl J Med. 2001;345(25):1809-1817.

interactions.mechanism: Dual RAAS blockade increases risk of hypotension, hyperkalemia, and renal failure.
interactions.recommendation: Do not combine. ONTARGET trial showed increased adverse events without benefit.
citations: ONTARGET Investigators. N Engl J Med. 2008;358(15):1547-1559.

interactions.mechanism: ACE inhibitors reduce lithium renal clearance, increasing toxicity risk.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor lithium levels closely. Dose reduction may be needed.
citations: Finley PR et al. J Clin Psychopharmacol. 1996;16(3):197-202.

interactions.mechanism: Both increase serotonin. Combined use risks serotonin syndrome (potentially fatal).
interactions.recommendation: Contraindicated. Never combine SSRIs with St. John's Wort. Allow 2-week washout.
citations: Lantz MS et al. J Geriatr Psychiatry Neurol. 1999;12(1):7-10.

interactions.mechanism: SSRIs deplete platelet serotonin; combined with NSAID GI effects, bleeding risk increases.
interactions.recommendation: Use with caution. Consider gastroprotection with a PPI if both necessary.
citations: Loke YK et al. Aliment Pharmacol Ther. 2008;27(1):31-40.

interactions.mechanism: Ginkgo antiplatelet properties combined with SSRI platelet effects increase bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: Use caution. Monitor for unusual bruising or bleeding.
citations: Izzo AA, Ernst E. Drugs. 2001;61(15):2163-2175.

interactions.mechanism: Curcumin may have mild serotonergic and antiplatelet effects interacting with SSRIs.
interactions.recommendation: Likely safe at dietary levels. High-dose supplements warrant monitoring.
citations: Kulkarni SK et al. Indian J Pharm Sci. 2008;70(4):455-462.

interactions.mechanism: Ibuprofen may block aspirin's irreversible platelet binding, reducing cardioprotection. Additive GI risk.
interactions.recommendation: Take low-dose aspirin 30 min before ibuprofen. Consider acetaminophen alternative.
citations: Catella-Lawson F et al. N Engl J Med. 2001;345(25):1809-1817.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal lithium clearance by up to 20%, causing elevated levels and toxicity.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid if possible. Monitor lithium levels within 5 days of starting NSAID.
citations: Phelan KM et al. Am J Psychiatry. 2003;160(8):1532-1533.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal clearance of methotrexate, increasing bone marrow suppression risk.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid with high-dose methotrexate. Low-dose (RA) requires close monitoring.
citations: Thyss A et al. Cancer Chemother Pharmacol. 1986;18(2):159-162.

interactions.mechanism: Chronic PPI use reduces gastric acid for calcium carbonate absorption; fracture risk increases.
interactions.recommendation: Use calcium citrate (acid-independent) instead of carbonate. Monitor bone density.

interactions.mechanism: Long-term PPIs cause hypomagnesemia through impaired intestinal Mg absorption (TRPM6).
interactions.recommendation: Monitor magnesium with chronic PPI use. Supplement if deficient. Consider PPI step-down.
citations: Hess MW et al. Aliment Pharmacol Ther. 2012;36(5):405-413.

interactions.mechanism: PPIs reduce gastric acid needed for ferric-to-ferrous iron conversion, reducing absorption.
interactions.recommendation: Co-administer vitamin C to enhance absorption. May need parenteral iron if refractory.
citations: Sharma VR et al. Intern Med J. 2019;49(3):336-343.

interactions.mechanism: Chronic PPIs may reduce calcium absorption, indirectly affecting vitamin D metabolism.
interactions.recommendation: Ensure adequate vitamin D supplementation on chronic PPI therapy.
citations: Heidelbaugh JJ. Curr Opin Gastroenterol. 2013;29(6):614-620.

interactions.mechanism: Grapefruit inhibits intestinal CYP3A4, increasing CCB bioavailability. Can cause hypotension.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid grapefruit with CYP3A4-metabolized CCBs. Monitor BP if consumption changes.
citations: Bailey DG et al. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 1998;46(2):101-110.

interactions.mechanism: Magnesium has mild vasodilatory/calcium-blocking properties, potentially additive with CCBs.
interactions.recommendation: Generally safe. Monitor BP with high-dose magnesium supplementation.
citations: Houston M. J Clin Hypertens. 2011;13(11):843-847.

interactions.mechanism: Non-dihydropyridine CCBs (diltiazem, verapamil) + beta-blockers risk bradycardia/AV block.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid verapamil/diltiazem with beta-blockers. Amlodipine/nifedipine generally safe.
citations: Edoute Y et al. Isr Med Assoc J. 2000;2(8):585-588.

interactions.mechanism: Calcium inhibits heme and non-heme iron absorption via shared transport mechanisms.
interactions.recommendation: Separate iron and calcium by 2+ hours. Take iron on empty stomach.
citations: Hallberg L et al. Am J Clin Nutr. 1991;53(1):112-119.
interactions.timing_advice: Take iron in the morning on empty stomach with vitamin C. Take calcium with lunch or dinner (at least 2 hours apart).

interactions.mechanism: Iron and zinc compete for DMT1 transporter. High-dose iron reduces zinc absorption.
interactions.recommendation: Supplement at different times of day. Dietary amounts unlikely to cause issues.
citations: Whittaker P. Am J Clin Nutr. 1998;68(2 Suppl):442S-446S.

interactions.mechanism: Calcium may modestly reduce zinc absorption through competitive transport inhibition.
interactions.recommendation: Separate high-dose supplements by 2 hours. Dietary intake is fine.
citations: Wood RJ, Zheng JJ. Am J Clin Nutr. 1997;65(6):1803-1809.

interactions.mechanism: Hypercalcemia sensitizes myocardium to digoxin, increasing fatal arrhythmia risk.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor serum calcium and digoxin levels. Avoid IV calcium in digitalized patients.
citations: Hauptman PJ, Kelly RA. Circulation. 1999;99(9):1265-1270.

interactions.mechanism: Hypomagnesemia increases digoxin toxicity by enhancing myocardial sensitivity.
interactions.recommendation: Maintain normal Mg levels. Supplementation may actually be protective.
citations: Kulick DL et al. Am Heart J. 1988;116(6 Pt 1):1591-1599.

interactions.mechanism: St. John's Wort induces P-glycoprotein/CYP3A4, reducing digoxin levels by up to 25%.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid combining. Monitor digoxin levels for 2 weeks if stopped.
citations: Johne A et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 1999;66(4):338-345.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce prostaglandin-mediated vasodilation, attenuating beta-blocker BP lowering.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor BP. Use lowest effective NSAID dose. Consider acetaminophen.
citations: Houston MC. Am J Med. 1991;90(5A):42S-47S.

interactions.mechanism: Vitamin D deficiency is more common in metformin users. Supplementation may improve insulin sensitivity.
interactions.recommendation: Consider checking vitamin D levels. Supplementation is safe and potentially beneficial.
citations: Kos E et al. Endocr Pract. 2012;18(2):179-184.

interactions.mechanism: Both have antiplatelet properties. Combined effect on bleeding time is additive but usually minor.
interactions.recommendation: Generally safe at standard doses. Watch for easy bruising or prolonged bleeding.
citations: Bays HE. Am J Cardiol. 2007;99(6A):35C-43C.

interactions.mechanism: Ginkgo PAF-inhibiting properties + aspirin significantly increase bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid combining. Discontinue ginkgo 2+ weeks before planned surgery.
citations: Bent S et al. J Gen Intern Med. 2005;20(7):657-661.

interactions.mechanism: ARBs reduce aldosterone-mediated K+ excretion. Supplemental potassium risks hyperkalemia.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid potassium supplements unless monitored. Check serum K+ regularly.
citations: Palmer BF. N Engl J Med. 2004;351(6):585-592.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs blunt ARB antihypertensive effect and increase acute kidney injury risk.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor BP and renal function. Use lowest effective NSAID dose.
citations: Dreischulte T et al. BMJ. 2015;350:h949.

interactions.mechanism: ARBs reduce lithium renal clearance similar to ACE inhibitors, increasing toxicity risk.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor lithium levels closely. Consider dose reduction.
citations: Finley PR et al. J Clin Psychopharmacol. 1996;16(3):197-202.

interactions.mechanism: Iron deficiency may impair vitamin D metabolism. No direct absorption competition.
interactions.recommendation: Both can be taken together safely. Address iron deficiency to optimize vitamin D.
citations: Luan H et al. Nutrients. 2020;12(11):3471.

interactions.mechanism: High-dose calcium may mildly compete with magnesium for absorption.
interactions.recommendation: Can be taken together at normal doses. Separate megadoses by 2 hours.
citations: Spencer H et al. J Am Coll Nutr. 1994;13(5):485-492.

interactions.mechanism: DOACs + NSAIDs significantly increase GI and intracranial bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid combination. Use acetaminophen instead. If unavoidable, add PPI.

interactions.mechanism: Dual antithrombotic therapy with DOAC + aspirin doubles major bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid unless specifically indicated (e.g., recent ACS). Use shortest duration.
citations: Alexander JH et al. N Engl J Med. 2011;365(8):699-708.

interactions.mechanism: SSRIs deplete platelet serotonin, adding bleeding risk on top of DOAC anticoagulation.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor for bruising/bleeding. Consider lower-risk antidepressants if possible.
citations: Quinn GR et al. Ann Intern Med. 2014;161(3):186-190.

interactions.mechanism: St. John's Wort induces P-glycoprotein/CYP3A4, reducing DOAC levels and anticoagulant efficacy.
interactions.recommendation: Contraindicated. Can cause treatment failure and thromboembolism.
citations: Wessler JD et al. J Am Coll Cardiol. 2013;61(25):2495-2502.

interactions.mechanism: Grapefruit inhibits CYP3A4/P-gp, increasing apixaban and rivaroxaban levels.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid grapefruit with apixaban/rivaroxaban. Dabigatran less affected.
citations: Frost CE et al. Br J Clin Pharmacol. 2015;79(5):838-846.

interactions.mechanism: Both increase serotonin. Combined use risks serotonin syndrome.
interactions.recommendation: Contraindicated. Never combine. Allow 2-week washout.
citations: Boyer EW, Shannon M. N Engl J Med. 2005;352(11):1112-1120.

interactions.mechanism: SNRIs impair platelet serotonin; combined with NSAID GI effects, bleeding risk increases 2-4 fold.
interactions.recommendation: Use caution. Consider PPI gastroprotection if both necessary.
citations: Meijer WE et al. Arch Intern Med. 2004;164(21):2367-2370.

interactions.mechanism: Ginkgo antiplatelet effects add to SNRI-related bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor for bruising/bleeding. Avoid combining.
citations: Izzo AA, Ernst E. Drugs. 2001;61(15):2163-2175.

interactions.mechanism: Combined serotonergic activity causes potentially fatal serotonin syndrome (hyperthermia, rigidity, autonomic instability).
interactions.recommendation: NEVER combine. Require 2-5 week washout between drugs (5 weeks for fluoxetine due to long half-life).
citations: Boyer EW, Shannon M. N Engl J Med. 2005;352(11):1112-1120.

interactions.mechanism: Combined serotonergic activity causes potentially fatal serotonin syndrome.
interactions.recommendation: NEVER combine. Require 2-week washout between drugs.
citations: Boyer EW, Shannon M. N Engl J Med. 2005;352(11):1112-1120.

interactions.mechanism: MAOIs block tyramine metabolism. Tyramine accumulates causing hypertensive crisis (headache, stroke risk).
interactions.recommendation: Strictly avoid aged cheese, cured meats, sauerkraut, soy sauce, draft beer, and fermented foods.
citations: Flockhart DA. J Clin Psychiatry. 2012;73(Suppl 1):17-24.

interactions.mechanism: MAOIs potentiate opioid effects. Meperidine specifically causes fatal serotonin syndrome.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid meperidine absolutely. Use other opioids with extreme caution.
citations: Gillman PK. Br J Anaesth. 2005;95(4):434-441.

interactions.mechanism: 5-HTP is a direct serotonin precursor. Combined with MAO inhibition causes serotonin syndrome.
interactions.recommendation: NEVER combine. Extremely dangerous.
citations: Turner EH et al. Pharmacol Ther. 2006;109(3):325-338.

interactions.mechanism: Iron forms insoluble chelates with fluoroquinolones, reducing antibiotic absorption by up to 90%.
interactions.recommendation: Take fluoroquinolone 2 hours before or 6 hours after iron.
citations: Nix DE et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 1989;46(6):700-705.
interactions.timing_advice: Take antibiotic 2 hours before or 6 hours after iron supplements.

interactions.mechanism: Calcium chelates fluoroquinolones, significantly reducing absorption.
interactions.recommendation: Take fluoroquinolone 2 hours before or 6 hours after calcium.
citations: Frost RW et al. Antimicrob Agents Chemother. 1992;36(4):830-832.
interactions.timing_advice: Take antibiotic 2 hours before or 6 hours after calcium.

interactions.mechanism: Magnesium chelates fluoroquinolones, reducing antibiotic absorption.
interactions.recommendation: Take fluoroquinolone 2 hours before or 6 hours after magnesium.
citations: Nix DE et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 1989;46(6):700-705.
interactions.timing_advice: Take antibiotic 2 hours before or 6 hours after magnesium.

interactions.mechanism: Zinc chelates fluoroquinolones, reducing antibiotic absorption.
interactions.recommendation: Take fluoroquinolone 2 hours before or 6 hours after zinc.
citations: Nix DE et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 1989;46(6):700-705.
interactions.timing_advice: Take antibiotic 2 hours before or 6 hours after zinc.

interactions.mechanism: Fluoroquinolones lower seizure threshold; NSAIDs further increase CNS excitability and seizure risk.
interactions.recommendation: Use with caution, especially in patients with epilepsy history.
citations: Halliwell RF et al. J Antimicrob Chemother. 1993;31(2):217-223.

interactions.mechanism: Dairy calcium chelates fluoroquinolones, reducing absorption 30-50%.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid dairy products within 2 hours of taking fluoroquinolone.
citations: Stass H et al. Clin Pharmacokinet. 2001;40(Suppl 1):57-62.
interactions.timing_advice: Take fluoroquinolone 2 hours before or 2 hours after dairy.

interactions.mechanism: Clarithromycin/erythromycin strongly inhibit CYP3A4, dramatically increasing statin levels and rhabdomyolysis risk.
interactions.recommendation: Suspend statin during clarithromycin/erythromycin course. Azithromycin is safer (minimal CYP3A4 effect).
citations: Jacobson TA. Am J Med. 2004;116(6):408-416.

interactions.mechanism: Macrolides inhibit CYP3A4/CYP1A2 and may reduce gut flora vitamin K synthesis, increasing INR.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor INR closely when starting/stopping macrolide. Dose may need reduction.
citations: Westergren T et al. Drug Saf. 2007;30(5):379-407.

interactions.mechanism: Macrolides inhibit CYP3A4, increasing CCB levels. Risk of hypotension/bradycardia.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor BP/HR. Consider azithromycin (less CYP3A4 effect).
citations: Wright AJ et al. CMAJ. 2011;183(3):303-307.

interactions.mechanism: Macrolides inhibit P-glycoprotein and kill gut bacteria that metabolize digoxin, causing toxic levels.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor digoxin levels. Consider temporary dose reduction.
citations: Bizjak ED, Mauro VF. Ann Pharmacother. 1997;31(9):1077-1079.

interactions.mechanism: Azole antifungals are potent CYP3A4 inhibitors, dramatically increasing statin levels and rhabdomyolysis risk.
interactions.recommendation: Suspend CYP3A4-metabolized statins (simvastatin, lovastatin, atorvastatin) during azole therapy.
citations: Kantola T et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 1998;64(1):58-65.

interactions.mechanism: Fluconazole strongly inhibits CYP2C9 (main warfarin metabolizer). INR can double within days.
interactions.recommendation: Reduce warfarin dose 25-50% when starting fluconazole. Monitor INR every 2-3 days.
citations: Black DJ et al. Ann Intern Med. 1996;124(1 Pt 1):107-109.

interactions.mechanism: Azole CYP3A4 inhibition increases CCB levels, risking hypotension.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor BP. Consider dose reduction of CCB during azole therapy.
citations: Nivoix Y et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2009;85(4):425-432.

interactions.mechanism: Azoles inhibit CYP3A4-mediated cyclosporine metabolism, causing nephrotoxic drug levels.
interactions.recommendation: Reduce cyclosporine dose 50% when starting azole. Monitor trough levels closely.
citations: Saari TI et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2006;80(2):126-135.

interactions.mechanism: Azoles inhibit CYP3A4 metabolism of tacrolimus, causing nephrotoxic and neurotoxic levels.
interactions.recommendation: Reduce tacrolimus dose. Monitor trough levels every 2-3 days.
citations: Venkataramanan R et al. Transplantation. 2002;73(7):1066-1072.

interactions.mechanism: Iron chelates tetracyclines, reducing antibiotic absorption 50-90%.
interactions.recommendation: Separate doses by 2-3 hours.
citations: Neuvonen PJ. Drugs. 1976;11(1):45-54.
interactions.timing_advice: Take tetracycline 2 hours before or 3 hours after iron.

interactions.mechanism: Calcium chelates tetracyclines, forming insoluble complexes.
interactions.recommendation: Separate doses by 2-3 hours. Avoid dairy near dosing time.
citations: Neuvonen PJ. Drugs. 1976;11(1):45-54.
interactions.timing_advice: Take tetracycline 2 hours before or 3 hours after calcium/dairy.

interactions.mechanism: Dairy calcium chelates tetracyclines, reducing absorption significantly.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid dairy products within 2-3 hours of tetracycline doses.
citations: Neuvonen PJ. Drugs. 1976;11(1):45-54.
interactions.timing_advice: Take tetracycline at least 2 hours before or after dairy.

interactions.mechanism: 5-HTP is a direct serotonin precursor. Combined with SSRIs causes excess serotonin and serotonin syndrome risk.
interactions.recommendation: Do not combine. Use one or the other, never both.
citations: Turner EH et al. Pharmacol Ther. 2006;109(3):325-338.

interactions.mechanism: 5-HTP combined with SNRIs causes additive serotonin elevation, risking serotonin syndrome.
interactions.recommendation: Do not combine.
citations: Turner EH et al. Pharmacol Ther. 2006;109(3):325-338.

interactions.mechanism: SAMe has serotonergic properties. Combined with SSRIs may increase serotonin syndrome risk.
interactions.recommendation: Use with caution. Start low dose if adding to SSRI. Monitor for agitation.
citations: Mischoulon D et al. J Clin Psychopharmacol. 2014;34(3):413-416.

interactions.mechanism: SAMe serotonergic activity adds to SNRI effects. Risk of serotonin excess.
interactions.recommendation: Use with caution. Monitor for agitation, tremor, hyperthermia.
citations: Mischoulon D et al. J Clin Psychopharmacol. 2014;34(3):413-416.

interactions.mechanism: CBD inhibits CYP2C19 and CYP3A4, significantly increasing warfarin levels and INR. Case reports of INR doubling.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid or monitor INR very closely. Warfarin dose reduction likely needed.
citations: Grayson L et al. J Thromb Thrombolysis. 2018;46(2):207-210.

interactions.mechanism: CBD inhibits CYP2D6 (metabolizes many SSRIs), potentially increasing SSRI levels and side effects.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor for increased SSRI side effects. Start CBD low if combining.
citations: Nasrin S et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2021;109(6):1525-1536.

interactions.mechanism: CBD may inhibit CYP3A4 to a modest degree, slightly increasing statin exposure.
interactions.recommendation: Likely safe at low CBD doses. Monitor for muscle symptoms at high doses.
citations: Nasrin S et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2021;109(6):1525-1536.

interactions.mechanism: Both lower blood glucose via complementary mechanisms. Combined use risks hypoglycemia.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor glucose closely if combining. May need metformin dose reduction.
citations: Yin J et al. Metabolism. 2008;57(5):712-717.

interactions.mechanism: Berberine inhibits CYP3A4 and may increase statin levels. Also has independent cholesterol-lowering effects.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor for muscle symptoms. Consider lower statin dose if combining.
citations: Guo Y et al. Atherosclerosis. 2014;232(2):381-387.

interactions.mechanism: Berberine inhibits CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein, significantly increasing cyclosporine levels and toxicity risk.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid combining. Cyclosporine dose adjustment difficult to predict.
citations: Xin HW et al. Eur J Clin Pharmacol. 2006;62(7):581-586.

interactions.mechanism: Ashwagandha may stimulate thyroid hormone production (increases T3 and T4), altering thyroid medication needs.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor TSH if combining. May need levothyroxine dose reduction.
citations: Sharma AK et al. J Altern Complement Med. 2018;24(3):243-248.

interactions.mechanism: Melatonin may enhance warfarin's anticoagulant effect, increasing bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor INR when starting/changing melatonin dose.
citations: Lissoni P et al. Thromb Res. 1998;91(1):43-47.

interactions.mechanism: Beta-blockers suppress melatonin secretion (via beta-1 receptors on pineal gland), which may worsen insomnia.
interactions.recommendation: Melatonin supplementation may be beneficial for beta-blocker insomnia.
citations: Scheer FA et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2012;91(3):609-616.

interactions.mechanism: Valerian has GABAergic properties. Combined with benzodiazepines causes excessive CNS depression and sedation.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid combining. Use one or the other for sleep.
citations: Miyasaka LS et al. Cochrane Database Syst Rev. 2006;4:CD004515.

interactions.mechanism: Valerian CNS-depressant effects are additive with opioids, increasing sedation and respiratory depression risk.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid combining.
citations: Miyasaka LS et al. Cochrane Database Syst Rev. 2006;4:CD004515.

interactions.mechanism: Milk thistle may mildly inhibit CYP3A4 and CYP2C9. Theoretical risk of increased statin levels.
interactions.recommendation: Likely safe at standard doses. Monitor for muscle symptoms.
citations: Wen Z et al. Drug Metab Dispos. 2008;36(9):1904-1916.

interactions.mechanism: Cyclosporine inhibits CYP3A4 and OATP1B1, dramatically increasing statin levels. High rhabdomyolysis risk.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid simvastatin/lovastatin. Use lowest doses of other statins. Pravastatin/fluvastatin preferred.
citations: Jacobson TA. Am J Med. 2004;116(6):408-416.

interactions.mechanism: Grapefruit inhibits CYP3A4, increasing cyclosporine to nephrotoxic levels.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid grapefruit entirely while on cyclosporine.
citations: Ducharme MP et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 1995;57(5):485-491.

interactions.mechanism: Combined CNS/respiratory depression is the leading cause of prescription overdose deaths.
interactions.recommendation: FDA black box warning: avoid concurrent use. If unavoidable, use lowest effective doses with close monitoring.
citations: Sun EC et al. BMJ. 2017;356:j760.

interactions.mechanism: Alcohol potentiates opioid CNS depression. Risk of fatal respiratory depression even with moderate drinking.
interactions.recommendation: Do not drink alcohol while taking opioids.
citations: Jones CM et al. MMWR Morb Mortal Wkly Rep. 2014;63(39):881-885.

interactions.mechanism: Tramadol specifically has serotonergic activity. Combined with SSRIs increases serotonin syndrome risk.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid tramadol + SSRI if possible. Other opioids have lower serotonin risk.
citations: Gillman PK. Br J Anaesth. 2005;95(4):434-441.

interactions.mechanism: GLP-1 agonists + sulfonylureas significantly increase hypoglycemia risk.
interactions.recommendation: Consider reducing sulfonylurea dose by 50% when starting GLP-1 agonist.
citations: Nauck MA et al. Diabetes Care. 2009;32(1):84-90.

interactions.mechanism: Combined use increases hypoglycemia risk. GLP-1 delayed gastric emptying also alters insulin PK.
interactions.recommendation: Reduce insulin dose when starting GLP-1 agonist. Monitor glucose closely.
citations: Buse JB et al. Lancet. 2009;374(9683):39-47.

interactions.mechanism: Additive diuretic effects increase dehydration, hypotension, and acute kidney injury risk.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor BP, hydration status, and renal function. Ensure adequate fluid intake.
citations: Perkovic V et al. N Engl J Med. 2019;380(24):2295-2306.

interactions.mechanism: SGLT2 inhibitors + insulin increase hypoglycemia risk. Also increases euglycemic DKA risk.
interactions.recommendation: Reduce insulin dose. Monitor glucose and ketones, especially during illness.
citations: Rosenstock J et al. Diabetes Care. 2015;38(9):1638-1642.

interactions.mechanism: Soy isoflavones may inhibit thyroid peroxidase and reduce levothyroxine absorption from the gut.
interactions.recommendation: Separate levothyroxine from soy products by 4+ hours. Monitor TSH if diet changes.
citations: Bell DS, Ovalle F. Ann Intern Med. 2001;134(10):946-947.
interactions.timing_advice: Take levothyroxine in the morning. Avoid soy for at least 4 hours.

interactions.mechanism: Modafinil induces CYP3A4, potentially reducing statin blood levels and efficacy.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor lipid levels. Statin dose may need adjustment. Rosuvastatin/pravastatin less affected.
citations: Robertson P et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2002;71(1):46-56.

interactions.mechanism: Modafinil induces CYP3A4, which metabolizes PDE5 inhibitors. May reduce tadalafil/sildenafil efficacy.
interactions.recommendation: May need higher PDE5i dose. Monitor effectiveness.
citations: Robertson P et al. Clin Pharmacol Ther. 2002;71(1):46-56.

interactions.mechanism: Bromantane may induce CYP3A4/CYP2D6 enzymes, potentially reducing statin blood levels.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor lipid levels if combining. Consider statins less dependent on CYP3A4 (rosuvastatin).
citations: Iezhitsa IN et al. Biull Eksp Biol Med. 2001;131(4):343-345.

interactions.mechanism: Bromantane's CYP3A4 induction may reduce PDE5 inhibitor blood levels and efficacy.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor PDE5i effectiveness. May need timing adjustment.
citations: Theoretical interaction based on known pharmacokinetic profiles.

interactions.mechanism: Bromantane modulates serotonin and dopamine. Theoretical interaction with SSRI serotonergic effects.
interactions.recommendation: Start low, monitor for serotonergic symptoms. Generally considered safe at standard doses.
citations: Theoretical interaction based on known pharmacokinetic profiles.

interactions.mechanism: CBD inhibits CYP3A4, potentially increasing PDE5 inhibitor blood levels. Risk of hypotension.
interactions.recommendation: Use caution. Start with lower PDE5i dose. Monitor blood pressure.
citations: Nasrin S et al. Drug Metab Dispos. 2021;49(12):1070-1080.

interactions.mechanism: Both are CYP3A4 substrates. Competition may alter blood levels of either drug.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor lipids and rapamycin levels. Use pravastatin/rosuvastatin (not CYP3A4-dependent).
citations: Mahalati K et al. Transplantation. 1999;68(4):510-513.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs cause sodium/water retention and reduce renal blood flow, worsening heart failure and increasing hospitalization risk.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs in heart failure. Use acetaminophen for pain.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Gislason GH et al. Arch Intern Med. 2009;169(2):141-149.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal perfusion via prostaglandin inhibition, accelerating kidney decline. Risk of acute kidney injury.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs if eGFR < 60. Use acetaminophen instead.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Zhang J et al. Kidney Int. 2018;94(2):363-369.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs inhibit COX-1, reducing protective gastric mucus. High risk of GI bleeding with ulcer history.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs. If unavoidable, use celecoxib + PPI.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Aspirin-exacerbated respiratory disease (AERD) affects 7-20% of asthmatics. NSAIDs can trigger severe bronchospasm.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Use with caution. Avoid if history of NSAID-triggered asthma. Acetaminophen is generally safe.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Rajan JP et al. J Allergy Clin Immunol Pract. 2015;3(3):305-312.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs inhibit platelet aggregation and increase bleeding time.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Avoid NSAIDs. Use acetaminophen for pain.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Schafer AI. Am J Med. 1999;107(6):635-645.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Beta-blockers block bronchial beta-2 receptors, causing bronchospasm. Even cardioselective agents carry risk.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Avoid non-selective beta-blockers (propranolol, carvedilol). Cardioselective (metoprolol, bisoprolol) with extreme caution only.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Salpeter SR et al. Ann Intern Med. 2002;137(9):715-725.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Beta-blockers may worsen airflow obstruction in COPD, though cardioselective agents may be tolerated.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Use cardioselective beta-blockers (bisoprolol, metoprolol) if indicated for heart failure/post-MI. Monitor lung function.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Dransfield MT et al. Lancet Respir Med. 2018;6(1):29-39.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Beta-blockers mask tachycardia (key hypoglycemia warning sign) and may impair glucose counter-regulation.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Monitor glucose more frequently. Cardioselective agents preferred. Educate on non-adrenergic hypoglycemia symptoms (sweating).
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Dungan K et al. Endocrinol Metab Clin. 2000;29(4):731-746.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Metformin accumulates when eGFR < 30 mL/min, increasing lactic acidosis risk (rare but fatal).
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Contraindicated if eGFR < 30. Reduce dose if eGFR 30-45. Monitor eGFR every 3-6 months.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Inzucchi SE et al. Diabetes Care. 2014;37(1):150-158.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Severe hepatic impairment reduces lactate clearance, increasing lactic acidosis risk with metformin.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Avoid in severe liver disease (Child-Pugh C). Use with caution in mild-moderate impairment.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: DeFronzo RA et al. N Engl J Med. 1995;333(9):574-579.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Previously contraindicated, now considered safe and even beneficial in stable heart failure. Only avoid in acute/unstable HF.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Safe in stable HF. Avoid in acute decompensated HF or with significant hypoxia.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Eurich DT et al. Circ Heart Fail. 2013;6(3):395-402.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Statins can cause hepatotoxicity. Active liver disease or unexplained persistent ALT elevation is a contraindication.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Avoid in active liver disease. Paradoxically, statins may benefit NAFLD. Check LFTs before starting.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Björnsson E et al. Hepatology. 2012;56(1):17-27.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Corticosteroids induce hepatic gluconeogenesis and peripheral insulin resistance. Dose-dependent hyperglycemia.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Monitor glucose in all diabetics on steroids. Insulin dose may need to double. Check HbA1c.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Hwang JL, Weiss RE. Endocrinol Metab Clin. 2014;43(1):75-102.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Corticosteroids are the most common cause of drug-induced osteoporosis. Bone loss is rapid in first 3-6 months.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Start calcium + vitamin D. Consider bisphosphonate if >3 months use. DEXA scan at baseline.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Van Staa TP et al. J Bone Miner Res. 2000;15(6):993-1000.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Corticosteroids impair mucosal healing and increase peptic ulcer risk, especially when combined with NSAIDs.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Consider PPI prophylaxis if concurrent NSAID or ulcer history.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Piper JM et al. Ann Intern Med. 1991;114(9):735-740.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Thiazides increase serum uric acid by competing for renal tubular secretion, triggering gout flares.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Consider alternative antihypertensive (CCB, ACE inhibitor). If thiazide essential, monitor uric acid and use prophylaxis.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Choi HK et al. BMJ. 2012;344:d8190.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Thiazides impair insulin secretion (hypokalemia-mediated) and increase insulin resistance.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Monitor glucose and HbA1c. Keep potassium normal. Low-dose thiazide (12.5mg HCTZ) has less metabolic impact.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Carter BL et al. Hypertension. 2008;52(6):1030-1037.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: SSRIs can trigger manic episodes in bipolar disorder. Risk of destabilization and rapid cycling.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Avoid monotherapy SSRI in bipolar. Must be combined with mood stabilizer if antidepressant needed.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Sachs GS et al. N Engl J Med. 2007;356(17):1711-1722.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: SSRIs deplete platelet serotonin, impairing aggregation. Increases bleeding risk in patients with bleeding disorders.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Use with caution. Consider mirtazapine or bupropion as alternatives.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Andrade C et al. J Clin Psychiatry. 2010;71(12):1565-1575.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: DOACs are renally cleared (dabigatran most, apixaban least). Accumulation in CKD increases bleeding risk.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Dose-adjust based on eGFR. Avoid dabigatran if eGFR < 30. Apixaban is safest in CKD (least renal clearance).
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Heidbuchel H et al. Europace. 2015;17(10):1467-1507.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Fluoroquinolones lower seizure threshold via GABA-A receptor antagonism.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Use with caution in epilepsy. Monitor for seizure activity. Consider alternative antibiotics.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Stahlmann R, Lode H. Drugs. 1999;58(Suppl 2):37-42.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Opioids depress central respiratory drive, worsening sleep apnea and increasing risk of fatal apnea events.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Avoid opioids if possible. If essential, use lowest dose with continuous pulse oximetry monitoring.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Chung F et al. Br J Anaesth. 2014;113(1):4-13.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Opioids depress respiratory drive. COPD patients already have compromised respiratory function.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Avoid strong opioids. If needed, use lowest effective dose with respiratory monitoring.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Vozoris NT et al. Eur Respir J. 2016;48(5):1477-1480.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Lithium is entirely renally excreted. Reduced clearance in CKD causes toxic accumulation.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Monitor levels and renal function very closely. Consider alternative mood stabilizer if eGFR declining.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Gitlin M. Int J Bipolar Disord. 2016;4(1):27.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: ACE inhibitors may initially raise creatinine but are renoprotective long-term. Risk of hyperkalemia in advanced CKD.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Continue unless creatinine rises >30% or K+ > 5.5. Monitor closely in first 2 weeks.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Bakris GL, Weir MR. Am J Med. 2000;109(2):164-167.

drug_condition_interactions.mechanism: Liver disease reduces clotting factor synthesis and warfarin metabolism, causing unpredictable INR and high bleeding risk.
drug_condition_interactions.recommendation: Use with extreme caution. Very frequent INR monitoring. DOACs may be safer alternatives in some cases.
drug_condition_interactions.citation: Tripodi A, Mannucci PM. N Engl J Med. 2011;365(2):147-156.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Metformin reduces ileal B12 absorption via calcium-dependent membrane action. 10-30% of users develop deficiency over years.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check B12 annually. Supplement if <300 pg/mL.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Aroda VR et al. J Clin Endocrinol Metab. 2016;101(4):1754-1761.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Metformin may modestly reduce folate absorption via same ileal mechanism.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Monitor folate if B12 is low. Supplement if deficient.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Sahin M et al. J Endocrinol Invest. 2007;30(5):880-884.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Statins can cause myopathy with elevated CK. Risk highest with simvastatin 80mg and drug interactions (CYP3A4 inhibitors).
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check CK if muscle symptoms. Baseline CK before starting.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Thompson PD et al. JACC. 2016;67(24):2395-2410.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Statins can cause hepatotoxicity (rare). ALT >3x ULN in 0.5-2% of patients.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check liver enzymes at baseline and if symptoms arise.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Björnsson E et al. Hepatology. 2012;56(1):17-27.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: AST may rise with ALT in statin hepatotoxicity. Less specific than ALT.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Monitor alongside ALT. Evaluate if >3x ULN.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Björnsson E et al. Hepatology. 2012;56(1):17-27.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Statins inhibit HMG-CoA reductase, reducing endogenous CoQ10 synthesis. May contribute to muscle symptoms.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Consider CoQ10 supplementation (100-200mg/day), especially if muscle symptoms.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Caso G et al. Am J Cardiol. 2007;99(10):1409-1412.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Chronic PPI use impairs intestinal Mg absorption via TRPM6 channel downregulation. Risk increases after 1+ year of use.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check magnesium annually on chronic PPI. Supplement if low.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Hess MW et al. Aliment Pharmacol Ther. 2012;36(5):405-413.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: PPIs reduce gastric acid needed for B12 release from food proteins. Deficiency develops over 2+ years of continuous use.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check B12 every 1-2 years on chronic PPI.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Reduced gastric acid impairs calcium carbonate absorption. Increased fracture risk with long-term use.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Use calcium citrate instead of carbonate. Monitor bone density.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: PPIs reduce ferric-to-ferrous iron conversion needed for absorption.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Co-administer vitamin C. Consider IV iron if refractory.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Sharma VR et al. Intern Med J. 2019;49(3):336-343.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: ACE inhibitors reduce aldosterone secretion, decreasing renal potassium excretion. Hyperkalemia risk especially with renal impairment.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check potassium within 1-2 weeks of starting, then every 6-12 months.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Palmer BF. N Engl J Med. 2004;351(6):585-592.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: ACE inhibitors reduce glomerular filtration pressure. Up to 30% creatinine rise is expected and acceptable.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check creatinine 1-2 weeks after starting. >30% rise warrants evaluation.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Bakris GL, Weir MR. Am J Med. 2000;109(2):164-167.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: ARBs reduce aldosterone via AT1 receptor blockade, same mechanism as ACEi.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check potassium within 1-2 weeks of starting, then periodically.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Palmer BF. N Engl J Med. 2004;351(6):585-592.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: ARBs reduce efferent arteriolar tone, may increase creatinine.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Monitor creatinine 1-2 weeks after initiation.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Bakris GL, Weir MR. Am J Med. 2000;109(2):164-167.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Thiazides block NaCl reabsorption in distal tubule. Hyponatremia in 4-11% of patients, higher in elderly.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check sodium within 1-2 weeks of starting and periodically.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Liamis G et al. Am J Kidney Dis. 2008;52(1):144-153.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Thiazides increase renal potassium excretion via increased distal sodium delivery and aldosterone stimulation.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check potassium regularly. Supplement or add K-sparing agent if low.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Ellison DH, Loffing J. Hypertension. 2009;54(3):313-320.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Thiazides impair insulin secretion (hypokalemia-mediated) and increase insulin resistance. New-onset diabetes in ~2-5%.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Monitor fasting glucose/HbA1c annually. Keep potassium normal.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Carter BL et al. Hypertension. 2008;52(6):1030-1037.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Thiazides compete with uric acid for renal tubular secretion.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Monitor uric acid if gout history. Consider alternative if symptomatic.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Choi HK et al. BMJ. 2012;344:d8190.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Thiazides enhance renal calcium reabsorption in distal tubule.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Usually beneficial (reduces kidney stones, helps bone density). Check calcium if symptoms of hypercalcemia.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Rejnmark L et al. J Intern Med. 2001;250(1):45-53.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Corticosteroids induce hepatic gluconeogenesis and peripheral insulin resistance. Dose-dependent hyperglycemia.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Monitor glucose in all patients on >7.5mg prednisone equivalent. Diabetics need dose adjustment.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Hwang JL, Weiss RE. Endocrinol Metab Clin. 2014;43(1):75-102.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Corticosteroids reduce intestinal calcium absorption and increase renal excretion. Major cause of drug-induced osteoporosis.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Supplement calcium + vitamin D. DEXA scan if >3 months use.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Van Staa TP et al. J Bone Miner Res. 2000;15(6):993-1000.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Mineralocorticoid activity of some corticosteroids increases renal potassium excretion.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Monitor potassium on high-dose or long-term steroids.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Bia MJ, DeFronzo RA. Am J Med. 1981;71(5):811-827.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: SSRIs can cause SIADH-mediated hyponatremia. Risk highest in elderly, women, and with concurrent diuretics.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check sodium within 2-4 weeks of starting, especially in elderly.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Jacob S, Bhatt RR. Am J Med. 2006;119(11):893-901.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: SSRIs deplete platelet serotonin stores, impairing platelet aggregation. Increases bleeding time.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Monitor for bruising/bleeding. Caution with concurrent anticoagulants.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Andrade C et al. J Clin Psychiatry. 2010;71(12):1565-1575.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Lithium concentrates in thyroid, inhibiting thyroid hormone release. Hypothyroidism in 20-30% of long-term users.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check TSH every 6-12 months on lithium therapy.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Bocchetta A et al. J Endocrinol Invest. 2001;24(5):334-338.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Lithium causes chronic tubulointerstitial nephropathy. GFR decline in 20-40% of patients over 10+ years.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check creatinine/eGFR every 6 months on lithium.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Gitlin M. Int J Bipolar Disord. 2016;4(1):27.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Methotrexate causes hepatotoxicity through folate antagonism. Liver fibrosis risk increases with cumulative dose.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check liver enzymes every 4-8 weeks. Hold if ALT >3x ULN.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Conway R et al. Ann Rheum Dis. 2015;74(12):e59.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Methotrexate suppresses bone marrow, causing pancytopenia. Risk highest with renal impairment and folate deficiency.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check CBC every 4-8 weeks. Supplement folate (1mg/day).
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Weinblatt ME. N Engl J Med. 2013;368(1):10-18.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Beta-blockers (especially non-selective) can mask hypoglycemia symptoms and impair insulin secretion. May worsen glycemic control.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Monitor glucose more frequently in diabetics. Cardioselective preferred.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Bangalore S et al. Lancet. 2007;370(9596):2313-2320.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Non-selective beta-blockers can raise triglycerides and lower HDL.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Monitor lipid panel. Consider carvedilol/nebivolol (lipid-neutral).
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Wiysonge CS et al. Cochrane Database Syst Rev. 2017;1:CD002003.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Warfarin's effect on INR is affected by diet (vitamin K), medications (CYP2C9/VKORC1), and illness. INR monitoring is essential.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check INR at least monthly. More frequently with diet/med changes.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Ansell J et al. Chest. 2008;133(6 Suppl):160S-198S.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: SGLT2 inhibitors lower glucose by blocking renal glucose reabsorption. Risk of euglycemic DKA (normal glucose but high ketones).
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Monitor ketones during illness, surgery, or fasting. Hold SGLT2i 3 days before planned surgery.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Goldenberg RM et al. Diabetes Care. 2016;39(12):2175-2180.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: SGLT2 inhibitors may modestly increase potassium via reduced distal sodium delivery and aldosterone suppression.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Monitor potassium, especially if combined with ACEi/ARB.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Perkovic V et al. N Engl J Med. 2019;380(24):2295-2306.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: GLP-1 agonists can mildly elevate pancreatic enzymes (lipase/amylase) without clinical pancreatitis.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Monitor if abdominal pain develops. Elevated lipase alone without symptoms does not require discontinuation.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Steinberg WM et al. Diabetes Care. 2017;40(7):966-972.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: DOACs increase bleeding risk. Monitoring hemoglobin detects occult bleeding (GI, genitourinary).
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check CBC every 6-12 months on DOAC therapy. Investigate unexplained hemoglobin drops.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Heidbuchel H et al. Europace. 2015;17(10):1467-1507.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: DOAC clearance depends on renal function. Dose adjustment required if creatinine rises / eGFR falls.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check creatinine/eGFR at least annually on DOACs. More frequently if >75 years or renal comorbidity.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Heidbuchel H et al. Europace. 2015;17(10):1467-1507.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: SNRIs (especially venlafaxine) can cause SIADH-mediated hyponatremia, similar to SSRIs.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Check sodium within 2-4 weeks of starting, especially in elderly.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: De Picker L et al. Psychosomatics. 2014;55(6):536-547.

drug_lab_interactions.mechanism: Fluoroquinolones can cause both hypo- and hyperglycemia, especially in diabetics. Gatifloxacin worst offender.
drug_lab_interactions.monitor: Monitor glucose in diabetics on fluoroquinolones.
drug_lab_interactions.citation: Park-Wyllie LY et al. Clin Infect Dis. 2006;42(5):657-668.
